name: webencodings

metadata.name: webencodings
metadata.summary: Character encoding aliases for legacy web content

documentation:
# python-webencodings

This is a Python implementation of the [WHATWG Encoding standard](http://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/).

- Latest documentation: <http://packages.python.org/webencodings/>
- Source code and issue tracker:
  <https://github.com/gsnedders/python-webencodings>
- PyPI releases: <http://pypi.python.org/pypi/webencodings>
- License: BSD
- Python 2.6+ and 3.3+

In order to be compatible with legacy web content
when interpreting something like `Content-Type: text/html; charset=latin1`,
tools need to use a particular set of aliases for encoding labels
as well as some overriding rules.
For example, `US-ASCII` and `iso-8859-1` on the web are actually
aliases for `windows-1252`, and an UTF-8 or UTF-16 BOM takes precedence
over any other encoding declaration.
The Encoding standard defines all such details so that implementations do
not have to reverse-engineer each other.

This module has encoding labels and BOM detection,
but the actual implementation for encoders and decoders is Python’s.

modules.name: webencodings
documentation:
webencodings
~~~~~~~~~~~~

This is a Python implementation of the `WHATWG Encoding standard
<http://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/>`. See README for details.

:copyright: Copyright 2012 by Simon Sapin
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for details.
coding: utf-8

functions.name: webencodings.lookup
documentation:
Look for an encoding by its label.
This is the spec’s [get an encoding](http://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-encoding-get) algorithm.
Supported labels are listed there.

```{eval-rst}

:param label: A string.
:returns:
    An :class:`Encoding` object, or :obj:`None` for an unknown label.
```

functions.name: webencodings._get_encoding
documentation:
Accept either an encoding object or label.

```{eval-rst}

:param encoding: An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
:returns: An :class:`Encoding` object.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown label.
```

functions.name: webencodings.decode
documentation:
Decode a single string.

```{eval-rst}

:param input: A byte string
:param fallback_encoding:
    An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
    The encoding to use if :obj:`input` does note have a BOM.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
:return:
    A ``(output, encoding)`` tuple of an Unicode string
    and an :obj:`Encoding`.
```

functions.name: webencodings._detect_bom
documentation: Return (bom_encoding, input), with any BOM removed from the input.

functions.name: webencodings.encode
documentation:
Encode a single string.

```{eval-rst}

:param input: An Unicode string.
:param encoding: An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
:return: A byte string.
```

functions.name: webencodings.iter_decode
documentation:
"Pull"-based decoder.

```{eval-rst}

then consumed on demand when the return value is.
:param fallback_encoding:
    An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
    The encoding to use if :obj:`input` does note have a BOM.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
:returns:
    An ``(output, encoding)`` tuple.
    :obj:`output` is an iterable of Unicode strings,
    :obj:`encoding` is the :obj:`Encoding` that is being used.
```

functions.name: webencodings._iter_decode_generator
documentation:
Return a generator that first yields the {obj}`Encoding`,
then yields output chukns as Unicode strings.

functions.name: webencodings.iter_encode
documentation:
“Pull”-based encoder.

```{eval-rst}

:param input: An iterable of Unicode strings.
:param encoding: An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
:returns: An iterable of byte strings.
```

classes.name: webencodings.Encoding
documentation:
Reresents a character encoding such as UTF-8,
that can be used for decoding or encoding.

```{eval-rst}
.. attribute:: name

    Canonical name of the encoding
```

```{eval-rst}
.. attribute:: codec_info

    The actual implementation of the encoding,
    a stdlib :class:`~codecs.CodecInfo` object.
    See :func:`codecs.register`.
```

classes.name: webencodings.IncrementalDecoder

methods.name: webencodings.IncrementalDecoder.decode
documentation:
Decode one chunk of the input.

```{eval-rst}

:param input: A byte string.
:param final:
    Indicate that no more input is available.
    Must be :obj:`True` if this is the last call.
:returns: An Unicode string.
```

“Push”-based decoder.

```{eval-rst}

The encoding to use if :obj:`input` does note have a BOM.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
```

classes.name: webencodings.IncrementalEncoder
documentation:
“Push”-based encoder.

```{eval-rst}

:param encoding: An :class:`Encoding` object or a label string.
:param errors: Type of error handling. See :func:`codecs.register`.
:raises: :exc:`~exceptions.LookupError` for an unknown encoding label.
```

```{eval-rst}
.. method:: encode(input, final=False)

    :param input: An Unicode string.
    :param final:
        Indicate that no more input is available.
        Must be :obj:`True` if this is the last call.
    :returns: A byte string.
```
